v ABSTRACT This project focuses on Data Exploration and Visualization using Microsoft Power BI. The main objective of the project is to analyze student performance data and generate meaningful educational insights through interactive dashboards and visual reports. The dataset used in this project is the Student Performance Dataset, which contains information related to student study hours, previous GPA, family income, parent education, school type, internet access, private tuition, and district-wise details. The data was imported into Power BI, where data cleaning and transformation processes were performed to prepare the dataset for analysis. After preprocessing, the dataset was modeled and connected to various visualization components in Power BI. Different charts, KPI cards, tables, slicers, and dashboards were developed to analyze student academic performance, study patterns, family background, and school-related factors. The project helps identify the relationship between study habits and academic results, the impact of parent education and family income, and variations in performance across districts and school types. This project demonstrates the practical application of business intelligence and data visualization tools for educational data analysis and supports data-driven decision- making in the academic domain. It also provides hands-on experience in data analytics, dashboard development, and interactive visualization using Power BI.

1 CHAPTER 1 INTRODUCTION In today’s digital era, educational institutions generate large amounts of data related to student performance, academic records, attendance, and learning activities, making data analysis and visualization essential for effective academic decision- making. This project, titled “Student Performance Dashboard using Power BI”, focuses on exploring and analyzing student performance data using modern business intelligence and data visualization tools. The project uses the Student Performance Dataset, which contains information related to study hours, previous GPA, family income, parent education, school type, internet access, private tuition, and district- wise details. The dataset is imported into Microsoft Power BI, where data cleaning and transformation processes are performed to prepare the data for analysis and visualization. Various interactive dashboards and reports are then developed using KPI cards, bar charts, pie charts, line charts, tables, and slicers to analyze student academic performance, study patterns, educational background, and district-wise distribution. The project helps identify factors influencing student performance and provides meaningful insights that support data-driven academic analysis and decision- making. It also provides practical knowledge in data analytics, dashboard development, report generation, and interactive visualization using Microsoft Power BI.

2 CHAPTER 2 OBJECTIVES 2.1 Analyze Student Performance Data Using Power BI The main objective of this project is to analyze student performance data using Microsoft Power BI and understand the various factors affecting academic performance. The project involves importing and managing the Student Performance Dataset in Power BI to perform efficient data analysis and visualization. The dataset contains information related to study hours, previous GPA, family income, parent education, school type, internet access, private tuition, and district-wise student details. Through this project, different academic and social factors influencing student performance are analyzed to understand overall educational trends and student behavior. The project also helps in understanding how business intelligence tools can be used for handling and analyzing educational data effectively. 2.2 Perform Data Exploration and Visualization Another important objective of this project is to perform data exploration and visualization to discover meaningful patterns and trends within the dataset. Data exploration involves examining the dataset to identify relationships between sales, profit, customer segments, regions, and product categories. Various visualization techniques such as bar charts, line charts, pie charts, maps, and KPI cards are used to represent the data graphically. Visualization helps in simplifying complex data and makes it easier to understand business performance. Through interactive reports and visual analysis, the project identifies sales growth trends, loss-making products, profitable categories, and region-wise performance. This process improves analytical thinking and supports data-driven decision-making. 2.3 Develop Interactive Dashboards in Microsoft Power BI The project also aims to design and develop interactive dashboards using Power BI. Interactive dashboards provide users with a clear and dynamic view of student data through visual elements and filters. Different dashboard components such

3 as KPI indicators, slicers, trend charts, tables, and category-wise reports are created to improve user interaction and analysis. Power BI allows users to filter and drill down into data for deeper insights into student performance and educational factors. The dashboard developed in this project enables users to analyze academic performance based on study hours, family income, school type, internet access, and district-wise student distribution. This objective helps in understanding the practical implementation of dashboard creation, report design, and visualization techniques using Power BI. 2.4 Generate Business Insights from Student Data The final objective of the project is to generate valuable educational insights from the student performance dataset. By analyzing the visual reports and dashboards, important academic findings can be identified, such as the impact of study hours on GPA, the role of parent education and family income, and the influence of internet access and private tuition on student performance. The project helps in understanding how educational institutions can use data analytics to improve learning outcomes and academic planning. The insights generated from the analysis support educational decision-making processes such as identifying areas requiring improvement, enhancing student support systems, and improving overall academic performance. This objective demonstrates the importance of business intelligence and data visualization tools in transforming raw educational data into meaningful and actionable information.

4 CHAPTER 3 REQUIREMENTS 3.1 Software Requirements The software requirements for this project include modern data analytics and visualization tools used for data processing, analysis, and dashboard development. Microsoft Power BI is used as the primary platform for importing the student performance dataset, performing data cleaning and transformation, and creating interactive dashboards and reports. Microsoft Excel or CSV file format is used for storing and preparing the dataset before importing it into Power BI. A modern web browser such as Google Chrome or Microsoft Edge is required to access Power BI services and online resources. The operating system used for the project can be Windows 10 or Windows 11. Software Requirements Table Software Purpose Microsoft Power BI Dashboard creation and data visualization Microsoft Excel / CSV Dataset preparation and editing Google Chrome / Microsoft Edge Accessing Power BI services Windows 10 / Windows 11 Operating system 3.2 Hardware Requirements The hardware requirements for this project are minimal because Power BI can efficiently handle small and medium-sized datasets on standard computer systems. A computer or laptop with a stable internet connection is sufficient to perform data analysis and dashboard development tasks. The system should have a minimum Intel Core i3 processor or equivalent, with at least 4 GB RAM for smooth operation. A storage capacity of 50 GB or more is recommended to manage datasets and project

5 files efficiently. Higher hardware specifications improve performance while handling multiple visualizations and reports. Hardware Requirements Table Hardware Component Specification Processor Intel Core i3 or above RAM Minimum 4 GB Storage 50 GB or above Internet Connection Stable broadband connection System Type Laptop or Desktop Computer

6 CHAPTER 4 ABOUT THE PROJECT 4.1 Introduction This project focuses on student performance data analysis and visualization using Microsoft Power BI. The main purpose of the project is to analyze academic data and convert raw information into meaningful insights using interactive dashboards and reports. The project uses a Student Performance Dataset that contains details related to study hours, previous GPA, family income, parent education, school type, internet access, private tuition, and district-wise student information. Power BI is used for data analysis, visualization, and dashboard development. This project demonstrates how business intelligence and data visualization tools help educational institutions analyze student data effectively and support academic decision-making processes. 4.2 Dataset Description The dataset used in this project is the Student Performance Dataset. The dataset contains information about student academic records and educational background. It includes multiple columns such as Student ID, Study Hours, Previous GPA, Family Income, Parent Education, School Type, Internet Access, Private Tuition, District, SSC Results, and HSC Results. The dataset helps in analyzing student performance from different perspectives, including study habits, educational support, family background, and district-wise performance. The dataset is stored in CSV format and imported into Power BI for further processing and analysis. 4.3 Data Upload to Lakehouse The dataset is imported into Microsoft Power BI for analysis and visualization. Initially, the CSV file is loaded into Power BI Desktop. Before creating reports and dashboards, column names and data types are verified and modified wherever necessary to maintain consistency and accuracy. The imported dataset is then prepared for analysis using Power BI tools and features. Power BI provides an

7 efficient environment for handling structured educational data and supports interactive analysis and visualization. 4.4 Data Cleaning and Transformation Data cleaning and transformation are important steps performed before creating visualizations and reports. The imported dataset is checked for missing values, duplicate records, incorrect data types, and formatting issues. Data transformation includes renaming columns, modifying categorical values, converting numerical data, and preparing the dataset for analysis. Additional calculated measures and fields are created to improve reporting and analytical capabilities. These transformations ensure that the dataset is accurate, structured, and ready for dashboard development in Power BI. 4.5 Semantic Model Creation After data cleaning and transformation, the dataset is organized and modeled within Power BI. Data modeling helps establish relationships between different data fields and improves reporting performance. Measures and calculated fields are created using DAX functions to generate meaningful insights and KPI values. The data model allows Power BI to efficiently process the dataset and supports interactive analysis through reports and dashboards. 4.6 Dashboard Development The dashboard is developed using Power BI to visually represent student academic performance and educational factors. Interactive dashboards are created to analyze study hours, GPA trends, family income distribution, parent education, school type comparison, and district-wise performance. Different visual components such as KPI cards, bar charts, pie charts, line charts, tables, and slicers are used in the dashboard. The dashboard is designed to provide a clear and user-friendly interface for analyzing student data. Interactive filters and drill-down features allow users to explore the data dynamically and gain deeper insights into academic performance.

8 4.7 Student Analysis Dashboard The Student Performance Analysis Dashboard focuses on analyzing overall student academic performance using various visualizations. KPI cards are used to display important indicators such as average GPA, total students, and average study hours. Bar charts and pie charts are used to compare parent education levels, school types, and district-wise distribution. Line charts are created to analyze the relationship between study hours and previous GPA. This dashboard helps in understanding academic performance patterns, educational support factors, and overall student learning behavior. 4.8 District and Educational Factors Analysis The District and Educational Factors Analysis section focuses on comparing student performance based on district, family income, internet access, and private tuition. Pie charts, tables, and bar charts are used to visualize district-wise student distribution and educational conditions. The analysis helps identify factors influencing student performance and highlights variations in academic outcomes across different educational backgrounds and regions. This analysis supports academic planning and helps identify areas requiring improvement in student support systems.

9 CHAPTER 5 VISUALIZATIONS AND REPORTS 5.1 KPI Cards KPI (Key Performance Indicator) cards are used in the dashboard to display important academic metrics in a simple and visually clear format. In this project, KPI cards are created to represent Average GPA, Total Students, Average Study Hours, and Student Performance Indicators from the student performance dataset. These cards provide a quick overview of overall academic performance and help users understand student statistics without analyzing large amounts of raw data. KPI cards are placed at the top of the dashboard to improve readability and highlight important information instantly. The use of KPI visuals improves dashboard effectiveness and supports quick academic analysis and decision-making. 5.2 Parent Education and School Type Analysis Parent Education and School Type Analysis is performed to analyze the educational background of students and compare public and private school performance. Bar charts and pie charts are used to compare parent education levels and school types available in the dataset. This analysis helps identify how educational background influences student performance. The visualization provides meaningful insights into the relationship between parent education and academic outcomes, supporting better understanding of educational and social factors affecting students. 5.3 District-wise Sales Analysis District-wise Performance Analysis focuses on comparing student distribution and academic performance across different districts. Pie charts, tables, and bar charts are used to display district-wise student details and study hour distribution. This analysis helps identify districts with higher or lower academic performance and variations in educational conditions. By understanding district-wise performance,

10 educational institutions can improve academic planning and student support strategies in specific regions. 5.4 Region-wise Profit Analysis Region-wise Profit Analysis focuses on comparing profit values across different geographical regions. Pie charts, bar charts, and map visualizations are used to display profit distribution among regions such as East, West, Central, and South. This analysis helps identify profitable and non-profitable regions within the business. By understanding regional performance, organizations can improve business strategies, marketing efforts, and resource allocation in specific areas. The analysis also supports decision-making related to regional sales expansion and operational improvements. 5.5 Interactive Filters and Slicers Interactive filters and slicers are implemented in the dashboard to improve user interaction and data exploration. Slicers are used for filtering data based on Year, Region, Category, and Customer Segment. These interactive elements allow users to analyze specific portions of the dataset dynamically without modifying the original data. Users can easily compare different regions, product categories, and time periods through the dashboard interface. The use of filters and slicers enhances dashboard usability and provides a more flexible and interactive reporting experience.
11 CHAPTER 6 OUTPUT SCREENSHOTS 6.1 Data Import in Power Bi 6.2 Data Modeling
12 6.3 Dashboard Overview 6.4 Student Analysis Dashboard
13 6.5 Final Report Output

14 CHAPTER 7 CONCLUSION The “Student Performance Dashboard using Power BI” project successfully demonstrates the process of analyzing and visualizing student academic data using modern business intelligence and data visualization tools. The project involved importing the Student Performance Dataset into Power BI, performing data cleaning and transformation, creating data models, and developing interactive dashboards and reports. Various visualizations such as KPI cards, line charts, bar charts, pie charts, tables, and slicers were created to analyze study hours, previous GPA, family income, parent education, school type, internet access, and district-wise student performance. The project helped identify important educational insights such as the relationship between study hours and academic performance, the impact of parent education and family income, and differences in student performance across districts and school types. The interactive dashboards improved data understanding and supported effective academic analysis through visual representation of student information. This project also provided practical knowledge in data analytics, dashboard development, report generation, and business intelligence reporting using Power BI. Overall, the project demonstrates how Power BI can transform raw student performance data into meaningful insights that support academic planning and educational decision-making.

15 CHAPTER 8 REFERENCES 1. Microsoft Power BI Documentation Microsoft Corporation. (2024). Provides guidance on dashboard creation, report development, data visualization, and interactive analytics using Power BI. 2. Microsoft Learn – Power BI Learning Resources Microsoft Corporation. (2024). Covers Power BI concepts, data modeling, visualization techniques, and report generation. 3. The Definitive Guide to DAX Marco Russo & Alberto Ferrari. (2023). Microsoft Press. Explains DAX formulas and calculated measures used in Power BI for data analysis and reporting. 4. Python for Data Analysis Wes McKinney. (2022). O’Reilly Media. Covers techniques for data cleaning, transformation, and data handling concepts relevant to analytics projects. 5. Information Dashboard Design: Displaying Data for At-a-Glance Monitoring Stephen Few. (2013). Analytics Press. Describes principles and best practices for effective dashboard and data visualization design. 6. Data Mining: Concepts and Techniques Jiawei Han, Micheline Kamber & Jian Pei. (2021). Morgan Kaufmann Publishers. Explains data analysis and pattern discovery concepts used in analytical projects. 7. Business Intelligence, Analytics, and Data Science: A Managerial Perspective Efraim Turban, Ramesh Sharda & Dursun Delen. (2022). Pearson Education. Covers business intelligence systems and data-driven decision-making techniques. 8. Microsoft Learn – Data Visualization in Power BI Microsoft Corporation. (2024). Explains Power BI visualizations, charts, KPI cards, slicers, and interactive dashboard features. 9. Student Performance Dataset on Kaggle Kaggle Dataset Repository. (2024). Provides the student performance dataset used for dashboard development and analysis in this project. 10. Power BI Community Documentation Power BI Community. (2024). Covers practical implementation of Power BI dashboards, reports, filters, and visualization techniques.
